g.title: reachscan_control_surface
g.text: ReachScan Control Surface: What Can Be Chosen, What Gets Recorded, What Gets Locked
ReachScan becomes claim-bearing when the object, sampling conditions, projection lens, and prediction rule are
declared before the run and then evaluated from raw receipts.

g.title: S1
g.text: • prefix : prompt · trace seed (single frozen trace)
— the contrast must be built by the run design

g.title: S2
g.text: • plan : depth fraction f · rollouts M · committed_len override

g.title: S4
g.text: how do I read each answer?

g.title: ART
g.text: • R_T(f) · dominant basin + mass · answer-field entropy
• yield / cap-hits · Wilson intervals · class-mass-by-depth · cost

g.title: VER
g.text: a declared READ of the data, not the data — computed from raw receipts;

g.title: LEGEND
g.text: chosen freely; written into the manifest / receipts (model, revision, plan, sampler, arm)
frozen once declared; turning it changes the hash — no post-hoc fudging

g.title: RAILS
g.text: what am I allowed to claim? (gates, not tuners)
governs the whole chain: object, sampling, lens, interpretation
